title: Function names should be preferred either as a call with a parameter list or with the & operator
description: Function names should be used either as a call with a parameter list or with the "&" operatorAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misrasuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoUsing a "bald" function name is likely a bug. Rather than testing the return value of a function with avoidparameter list, it implicitly retrieves the address of that function in memory. If that’s truly what’s intended, then it should be made explicit with the use of the&(address-of) operator.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant:
bool isReady();
int status = isReady;  // Noncompliant: assigns function address instead of calling

checks.message: Function name used without parentheses or & operator - use function() or &function
